name: MediaDash
description: Keeps your library lean and playable: finds duplicate copies, broken files, oversized encodes and unwanted language tracks, then fixes them safely. Dry-run mode and a recycle bin protect your media by default.
overview: Keeps your library lean and playable.

versions.changelog: The 'Run fixes now' button used to appear to do nothing when every attempted fix failed fast (typical on a Linux server where the jellyfin user doesn't own the media files) - the progress bar just flashed and the user was left to click through Errors to find out what happened. Fix runs now finish with a dashboard alert summarising attempted / succeeded / failed and the dominant reason, with reason-specific instructions (e.g. the exact chown command to grant Jellyfin write access). Manually-approved issues under types set to 'Detect only' now actually run - previously they were silently filtered out. The Overview drive list now always shows the drive the recycle bin lives on, even when it's on a Docker overlay / tmpfs / btrfs subvolume that DriveInfo filters out, so you notice when the bin is filling your system disk. New 'Check library access' button in first-run setup that probes read+write on every library folder and reports pass/fail per folder with a plain-language reason, so ownership/ACL problems get caught before you approve anything.

Media sorter now surfaces a clear, actionable error message on the Errors tab when a target folder is misconfigured (doesn't exist, sits outside every Jellyfin library, or isn't writable by the Jellyfin user) instead of silently creating hundreds of unfixable issues. The fixer also distinguishes those cases at fix time and pre-checks free disk space for cross-volume moves. 'Empty recycle bin now' used to freeze the UI on large bins and silently fail on older ApiClient builds - it now runs in the background with a determinate progress bar, and any per-batch permission or I/O errors show as a dashboard alert instead of tanking the whole operation on the first bad file.

New 'Misplaced files' fix type: MediaDash spots a movie sitting in your TV folder (or a TV episode sitting in Movies) and moves it into the correct folder. Set the two target folders under Settings > Libraries; classification uses Jellyfin's own metadata by default with a filename-pattern fallback (SxxExx / NxN => TV). LibraryGuard refuses moves outside your Jellyfin libraries. Optional new toggle to rename files after a successful re-encode to a canonical form: 'Name (Year) - 1080p.mkv' for movies, 'Series - S03E14 - 1080p.mkv' for TV episodes. Every primary button and settings chip now shows a tooltip on hover.

Recycle bin pre-checks free space on cross-volume moves and Overview warns when the bin lives on a different volume than your libraries. Issues tab now shows queued items alongside detected ones (they no longer vanish on approval) and refreshes live during a fix run so items disappear as they succeed. New 'Retry all fixes' button on the Errors tab clears the buffer and reruns the queue.

Polish pass. File browser and fix task catch UnauthorizedAccessException with an actionable message (points at Jellyfin's user needing write access) instead of a generic 500; denied fixes also show in History. New Encode preset dropdown (Faster/Balanced/Best) for software re-encodes. Quality scanner ignores files smaller than a configurable threshold (100 MB default) and skips HDR content by default so naive transcodes don't destroy HDR metadata. Errors tab collapses repeated identical entries into a single row with a count. New live filter-by-filename search on the Issues tab.

Linux hotfix. /Status endpoint crashed on Linux because SystemStats referenced Windows-only PerformanceCounter at class-load time. Now loaded lazily so the type only resolves inside the Windows branch. Sample() also gains a top-level try/catch that surfaces future failures to the Errors tab instead of taking the tab down silently.

Multi-GPU monitoring (NVIDIA / Windows PDH / Linux sysfs, per-card) with per-GPU cells. New Preferred GPU dropdown routes re-encodes to a specific card via ffmpeg -gpu for NVENC/AMF. Windows GPU counter fix: PDH counters are cached across polls and aggregated max-across-engines so numbers match Task Manager. Overview polls every 3s while visible, and metric cells update in place so the tab no longer flickers. New Errors tab with red badge exposes swallowed plugin exceptions. Approve refreshes status so 'nothing to fix' isn't stale. Approve-all button restyled and aligned. 'Space you could reclaim' card no longer overhangs its container.

GPU monitoring now covers AMD and modern Intel iGPUs on Linux via /sys/class/drm sysfs when nvidia-smi isn't available. GPU cell shows the data source (NVIDIA / Linux sysfs). Sidebar icon changed from generic 'dashboard' to 'auto_fix_high' so MediaDash stands out at a glance.

System performance card now reports whole-host CPU and RAM (Windows via GetSystemTimes/GlobalMemoryStatusEx, Linux via /proc). macOS and containers show N/A. Drives card now lists every fixed drive on the host, with a badge on library-hosting drives.

Overview leads with a live System performance card (Jellyfin CPU%, memory, NVIDIA GPU% via nvidia-smi) at the top, then Library drive storage, then everything else. Themed to match the rest of the plugin with greenâ†’amberâ†’red bar colouring.

Overview polish. Scan/Fix buttons now cancel the running task (red Stop state) with a matching themed gradient look. Current file shown under the progress bar. Per-drive storage card. No more 3-second flicker while scanning/fixing. Fix run processes smallest files first so early completions free disk space for the big ones.

Files tab restyled to match the rest of the plugin: 14px rounded cards, Jellyfin-blue accent on headers/hover, tabular numbers for size/date, breadcrumb pill, refined bulk action bar and folder-picker modal. Visual only, no functional changes.
Files tab: multi-select with bulk delete/move/download, filter box, and a modal folder picker that replaces the raw path prompt for move/copy operations.

Files tab: sortable columns (Name, Size, Modified), directories stay on top. Up button fixed at library roots. Issues tab: click a file path to open its folder in the Files tab; new 'Approve all N issues (all types)' button when no type filter is set.
Files tab: drag-and-drop uploads. Drop one or many files onto the list to upload into the current folder. Multi-file selection via the Upload button too. Admin-only, same safety as before.

New Files tab: browse, upload, download, rename, move, copy and delete files inside your library folders. Deletes go to the recycle bin. Every operation is refused if it targets anything outside a configured library.

Fewer false-positive unplayable flags; less aggressive re-encode disk check so batches can proceed on tighter drives; live progress bar during transcodes; helpful hint when 'Run fixes now' has nothing to do; new 'Reset scan data' button in Settings > Advanced to recover from stuck state without reinstalling. Existing installs get a one-time decode-cache wipe so the playability fix takes effect immediately.
